2606.26062.title: When Certainty Is an Artifact: Keyword Lexicon Blindness and the (Mis)Measurement of Rhetorical Stance
2606.26062.paper_summary: Can a statistically significant, large-effect-size finding in computational social science be entirely an artifact of the measurement instrument? We present a case where the answer appears to be yes. Analyzing 85 interviews across four public intellectuals (2016--2026), we find a robust negative-affect/emphatic-certainty lexical co-occurrence pattern under keyword-based scoring ($r = 0.72$--$0.93$, $p < 0.01$ for all four speakers). Replacing keyword counting with LLM-based zero-shot semantic classification on the complete diarized corpus (32,625 sentences) dramatically reduces this correlation: Dalio's $r = 0.851$ drops to $r = 0.206$, with two speakers showing negative $r(\text{neg}, \text{emphatic})$ and one showing null. In contrast, the LLM reveals a strong negative-hedging coupling across speakers -- Rogoff's $r(\text{neg}, \text{hedged}) = 0.875$ ($p = 0.001$) and Zeihan's $r(\text{neg}, \text{hedged}) = 0.722$ ($p = 0.008$) -- consistent with the conventional expectation that pessimistic discourse attracts hedging, not certainty. Sentence-level error analysis traces this discrepancy to three structural failure modes in keyword lexicons -- syntactic blindness, polysemy blindness, and categorical absence -- illustrated through cases where keyword counting inverts semantic meaning (e.g., ''never absolutely totally confident'' scored as high-certainty). We argue that keyword lexicons measure a universal lexical co-occurrence tendency -- negative discourse naturally attracts emphatic vocabulary -- that is orthogonal to, and can systematically invert, rhetorical stance. Treating keyword counts as measurements of epistemic certainty is a category error: a finding that appears to be about a speaker's psychology may be entirely about the counting of words.
2606.26062.paper_summary_zh: 一個在計算社會科學中具有統計顯著性和大效應量的發現，是否完全可能是測量工具的產物？我們提出了一個案例，答案似乎是肯定的。通過分析四位公共知識分子（2016--2026）中的85次訪談，我們發現了一種穩健的負面情感/強調確定性詞彙共現模式，基於關鍵詞的計分結果為 ($r = 0.72$--$0.93$, $p < 0.01$ 對於所有四位講者)。用基於LLM的零樣本語義分類替代關鍵詞計數，對完整的逐字稿語料庫（32,625句）顯著降低了這種相關性：Dalio的$r = 0.851$降至$r = 0.206$，兩位講者顯示出負的$r(\text{neg}, \text{emphatic})$，而一位顯示為零。相比之下，LLM揭示了講者之間強烈的負向對沖耦合——Rogoff的$r(\text{neg}, \text{hedged}) = 0.875$ ($p = 0.001$) 和Zeihan的$r(\text{neg}, \text{hedged}) = 0.722$ ($p = 0.008$)——這與傳統預期一致，即悲觀的話語吸引對沖，而非確定性。句子級別的錯誤分析將這一差異追溯到關鍵詞詞彙中的三種結構性失效模式——語法盲點、多義性盲點和類別缺失——通過關鍵詞計數顛倒語義意義的案例來說明（例如，“從不絕對完全自信”被計為高確定性）。我們認為，關鍵詞詞彙測量的是一種普遍的詞彙共現趨勢——負面話語自然吸引強調詞彙——這與修辭立場是正交的，並且可以系統性地顛倒。將關鍵詞計數視為認識論確定性的測量是一種類別錯誤：一個看似關於講者心理的發現，可能完全是關於詞語計數的。
2606.26062.author: Bo Chen et.al.
